title: IT Support Engineer
jd_text:
SupportTech Ltd is an IT service provider dedicated to delivering top-notch support to our clients. Our team is passionate about technology and customer service.

**Responsibilities:**
- Provide technical support for various applications and systems using Nodejs.
- Assist in troubleshooting hardware and software issues remotely.
- Document issues and resolutions to improve knowledge base.
- Communicate with users to understand their needs and provide solutions.

**Requirements:**
- Familiarity with node.js is essential.
- Strong communication skills and customer-oriented mindset.
- Previous experience in an IT support role is a plus but not mandatory.

**Benefits:**
- Annual salary of £60,000-£80,000.
- Work from anywhere policy - 100% remote!
- Ongoing training and professional development opportunities.
label: maybe

title: Full Stack Engineer
jd_text:
At TechNinja Solutions, we pride ourselves on delivering cutting-edge software solutions to a diverse clientele. We are seeking a Full Stack Engineer who is proficient in both frontend and backend technologies to join our remote team.

Responsibilities:
- Develop and maintain full-stack applications using Node.js and javascript.
- Collaborate with designers and product managers to implement user-friendly interfaces.
- Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code.
- Conduct code reviews and ensure high-quality standards.

Requirements:
- Proven experience as a Full Stack Engineer.
- Proficiency in Node.js and JavaScript/TS is a must.
- Experience with RESTful APIs and database management.
- Strong problem-solving skills.

Nice to have:
- Familiarity with DevOps practices.

Benefits:
- Fully remote working arrangement.
- Salary range of £60,000 - £80,000.
- Health insurance and wellness programs.
- Flexible vacation policy.
label: maybe

title: Project Manager
jd_text:
Global Network Solutions is dedicated to providing exceptional networking services and solutions to businesses worldwide. Our innovative approach sets us apart in a competitive market. 

**Responsibilities:**  
- Oversee project timelines, budgets, and deliverables.  
- Coordinate with cross-functional teams, ensuring successful implementation of Nodejs projects.  
- Maintain regular communication with stakeholders and clients.  
- Analyze project risks and develop mitigation strategies.  

**Requirements:**  
- Experience with Node.js and Javascript/TS.  
- Proven track record in project management, preferably in tech.  
- Strong organizational and leadership abilities.  
- Familiarity with Agile methodologies is nice to have.  

**Benefits:**  
- Fully remote working options – work from anywhere!  
- Salary between £60,000 and £80,000 per year.  
- Health and wellness programs.  
- Opportunities for career advancement and growth.  

*Become part of our dynamic team today!*
arr_raw: Fully remote working options – work from anywhere!
label: good_fit

title: Scrum Master
jd_text:
Agile Dynamics is a consulting firm that specializes in helping organizations implement agile methodologies. We are looking for a Scrum Master to lead our teams in delivering high-quality software solutions.

Responsibilities:
- Facilitate daily stand-ups, sprint planning, and retrospectives.
- Remove impediments that hinder team progress in projects.
- Mentor team members on agile practices and principles.

Requirements:
- Certified Scrum Master (CSM) certification is a plus.
- Experience with nodejs and agile project management tools.
- Strong interpersonal and communication skills.

Benefits:
- Office-based position with a vibrant team.
- Salary: £60,000-£80,000.
- Health insurance and retirement plans.
- Opportunity to work on innovative projects in a collaborative environment.
arr_raw: Office-based position with a vibrant team.
label: bad_fit

title: Engineering Manager
jd_text:
FutureTech Labs is at the forefront of developing innovative technology solutions for various industries. We are currently seeking an Engineering Manager to lead our talented engineering team.

Responsibilities:
- Manage and mentor a team of engineers, fostering a culture of innovation.
- Oversee project timelines and deliverables, ensuring quality standards are met.
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to align engineering efforts with business goals.
- Identify and implement process improvements for efficiency.

Requirements:
- Proven experience in a managerial role within a tech environment.
- Proficiency in Node.js is essential.
- Strong leadership skills and a passion for team development. 
- Familiarity with Agile methodologies is a nice to have.

Benefits:
- 100% remote working, giving you the flexibility to work from anywhere.
- Salary range: £60,000-£80,000.
- Generous vacation policy and opportunities for training.
- A collaborative and innovative work culture.
arr_raw: 100% remote working, giving you the flexibility to work from anywhere.
label: maybe

title: Senior Software Engineer
jd_text: Role: Senior Software EngineerLocation: Canary Wharf, Central LondonSalary: £70,000-£75,000 Plus Hybrid / Flexible Working, Company Bonus, Generous Pension and more.We are working with a well-funded, mission-driven organisation in the renewable energy space looking to hire a Senior Software Engineer into a core engineering team.You’ll be working on complex, large-scale platforms supporting critical infrastructure at a national level.This is a high-impact engineering environment where strong technical input genuinely matters.You’ll be responsible not just for building software, but for contributing to technical direction, design decisions, and engineering standards across the team.Key ResponsibilitiesBuild and scale high-performance, cloud-based applicationsDevelop backend services using Python (Django)Work across TypeScript / JavaScript where neededContribute to technical design, architecture, and engineering best practicesProvide technical leadership, supporting and mentoring other engineersImprove system performance, scalability, and reliabilityCollaborate with Product, QA and DevOps to deliver featuresRequirementsStrong experience with Python (Django)Experience with JavaScript / TypeScriptExperience working in cloud environments (AWS, Azure or GCP)Strong understanding of APIs, microservices, and scalable systemsExperience contributing to technical design or architecture decisionsAbility to provide technical leadership and mentor othersExperience working in modern engineering environments (CI/CD, Agile)Desirable SkillsExperience with React, Node.js, Terraform or CeleryStrong database design and optimisation experienceIf you’re a Senior Engineer looking for a role combining hands-on development, technical ownership, and real-world impact, this is worth a conversation. Hit apply today!KeywordsSenior Software Engineer, Python Developer, Django, Flask, JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, AWS, Azure, GCP, Cloud Engineer, Backend Developer, Full Stack Developer, Microservices, API Development, CI/CD, DevOps, Terraform, Celery, Data Engineering, Renewable Energy Tech, Technical Leadership, Software Architecture, Scalable Systems, Leeds Jobs, London Jobs, Hybrid Jobs, Tech Jobs UK
tech_raw: Python (Django), JavaScript / TypeScript, React, Node.js, Terraform, Celery
label: good_fit

title: Senior DevOps Engineer - AWS Infrastructure Architect
jd_text:
Compensation: £72k
About DeliveryAppDeliveryApp is in an exciting phase of rapid growth. As we scale our operations and technology, we are expanding our engineering team to build the next generation of our platform. We are looking for a highly capable Senior DevOps Engineer to step in and take full ownership of our cloud infrastructure. You will not just be managing servers; you will be driving the architecture, making critical technical decisions, and leading the implementation of our DevOps capabilities from the ground up.The Mission You will be architecting highly reliable, scalable, and secure AWS infrastructure designed to support high-impact, real-world logistics operations. Expect to tackle complex challenges involving high-availability architecture, seamless deployment pipelines, and proactive disaster recovery to keep our core network running flawlessly.The Role This role owns infrastructure reliability, scalability, and cost efficiency. We are looking for an engineer with deep Infrastructure-as-Code maturity who will take complete ownership of designing, deploying, and maintaining sophisticated AWS environments that drive real value for the business.Core StackCloud: AWS (EC2, EKS, RDS, Lambda, S3, CloudFront)Infrastructure-as-Code: Terraform / CloudFormationContainerization: Docker / KubernetesCI/CD: GitHub Actions, JenkinsObservability: CloudWatch, Prometheus, GrafanaWhat Sets This Role ApartSecurity: A strong, security-first mindsetFinOps: Advanced cost optimization strategies and FinOps awarenessResilience: Deep expertise in disaster recovery and high-availability architectureAutomation: High maturity in Infrastructure-as-Code practicesRequirementsExperience: 5+ years of dedicated DevOps experienceScale Management: Proven track record managing production-scale AWS infrastructureIncident Response: Strong troubleshooting and resolution skills during live incidentsCommunication: Excellent and fluent English speaking skills are absolutely essential. You must have the ability to communicate clearly and effectively across both engineering and product teamsWorking Model: A minimum of 4 days a week on-site is required for deep collaboration, strategic planning, and essential on-site presence during major deployments or outagesCompensationSalary: Competitive and negotiable based on experience
arr_raw: A minimum of 4 days a week on-site is required for deep collaboration, strategic planning, and essential on-site presence during major deployments or outages
tech_raw: AWS (EC2, EKS, RDS, Lambda, S3, CloudFront), Terraform / CloudFormation, Docker / Kubernetes, GitHub Actions, Jenkins, CloudWatch, Prometheus, Grafana
label: bad_fit
